**COMP0067 App Engineering**

**February 10, 2026**

Department of Computer Science

University College London

## 1\. Similar Application Review

This section reviews existing platforms that partially overlap with the proposed CV concierge library. While none of these systems fully address the problem space, they provide useful points of comparison in terms of recruitment flow, consent handling, and discovery models.

### 1.1 LinkedIn

LinkedIn operates as a large-scale professional social network using a freemium model. Users can upload a CV-like profile and control visibility at a basic public or private level.

#### 1.2.1 Strengths

1. Strong industry adoption and brand trust.

2. Passive discovery through recruiter search.

3. Messaging and networking features are mature.

#### 1.2.2 Limitations

1. No institution-level verification for student status.

2. Visibility controls are coarse and binary rather than granular.

3. Students cannot give consent to specific organisations only.

4. Recruiter access prioritises paid subscriptions rather than ethical data access.

5. The platform is not specialised for “university students” or for students wanting a career in academia. 

### 1.2 Handshake

Handshake is a university-focused recruitment platform widely used in higher education institutions. It follows an application-driven model where students actively apply for roles.

#### 1.3.1 Strengths

1. Strong university integration and student verification

2. Trusted by institutions and career services

3. Centralised job postings

#### 1.3.2 Limitations

1. No recruiter-led discovery without a job posting

2. Students must apply to be visible

3. Limited consent flexibility beyond application submission

4. CVs are not browsable assets

5. Recruitment interaction is one-directional

### 

### 1.3 Otta

Otta is a modern job board focused primarily on technology roles. It uses profile matching and ation algorithms.

#### 1.3.1 Strengths

1. Clean user experience

2. Skills-based matching

3. Transparency around employer interest

#### 1.3.2 Limitations

1. Narrow industry focus

2. No academic or institutional verification

3. Limited consent controls

4. CV visibility is tied to platform matching logic

5. Not suitable for charities or non-tech employers

### 1.4 Competitive Gap Summary

Across all reviewed platforms, several consistent gaps emerge:

1. Lack of granular consent at the individual employer level

2. No CV library model that supports passive discovery with privacy

3. Limited support for early-stage students and academic credentials

4. Absence of institution-governed access control

5. Recruiter-centric monetisation rather than student protection

The proposed platform directly addresses these gaps by positioning the CV as a controlled digital asset rather than an application attachment. By allowing students to explicitly manage who can view their CV and when, the system introduces a privacy-first recruitment model aligned with academic and ethical standards.

#### 

#### 

#### 2.3.3 Figma Prototype for Student/Recruiter/Admin user groups

Link: [Figma](https://www.figma.com/design/J3KdeHH9QG17pBhfZybpu4/Report-2---FIGMA?node-id=0-1&t=vdeC4gx6Waobh6rE-1)

### 2.4 Evaluation

Report 1 Section 1.3 established **accessibility and inclusion** as core design principles for the platform. These requirements cover optimising for older devices with limited compute power, keyboard navigation and ESL considerations \- informing heuristic evaluation and prototype design decisions. Screen reader compatibility and assistive technology testing will be completed at the UAT phase to validate technical implementation.

#### 2.4.1 Heuristic Evaluation

**Evaluation Setup:** 5 team members independently evaluated the Figma prototype (student, recruiter, Silver/Gold/Platinum, admin interfaces) against Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics using a 0-4 severity scale. Evaluation assessed effectiveness, efficiency, safety, utility, learnability and memorability across all user roles and tier levels. Findings were consolidated with duplicates removed, identifying 40 total issues documented below by interface type.

##### 2.4.1.1 Student

| Heuristic | Problem | Original Design | Solution | \# |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| H1: Visibility of system status | No upload confirmation visible | CV upload page shows a button, but it's unclear if the upload succeeded | Uploaded CV appears in the CV Library section withthe  filename shown | 4 |
| H2: Match between system and real world | Academic section formal labels | "Academic Achievements" and "Additional Academic Information" terminology | Simplified labels visible in navigation as "Academic Experience" | 2 |
| H3: User control and freedom | Cannot switch active CV | Multiple CVs are uploadable, but no clear active CV designationis  visible | The CV Library section shows uploaded files with management options | 3 |
| H4: Consistency and standards | Navigation labels inconsistent | Left sidebar uses mix of terminology across different sections | Standardised navigation structure visible across all student pages | 2 |
| H5: Error prevention | Account deletion is visible without safeguards | The Delete Account section in Account Management shows a warning text list | Red warning box with 5-point checklist before deletion button | 3 |
| H7: Flexibility and efficiency of use | Profile updates multi-step | Students must navigate between the Academic, Skills, and CV pages separately | Profile completion indicator (58%) is visible, showing progress centrally | 2 |
| H8: Aesthetic and minimalist design | Access History cluttered table | The Complete Access Log table shows 8 columns of data | The table displays the company, recruiter name, role, date, and status in organised rows | 2 |
| H9: Pre-empt user errors | CV upload format unclear | The upload section lacks file type specifications visible | "Upload your CV" heading is present, but no format guidance is shown | 3 |

##### 2.4.1.2 Admin 

| Heuristic  | Problem | Original Design | Solution | \# |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| H1: Visibility of system status | Pending approvals not prominent | Dashboard shows "12 Companies" metric but pending count unclear | Highlight pending approvals separately with "12 Pending" alert badge | 3 |
| H2: Match between system and real world | Status badges colour-only | Table rows show green/blue/yellow badges without text labels | Verified: badges show "Active", "Pending" text \- adequate labelling | 1 |
| H3: User control and freedom | Individual approval workflow | Partners table requires row-by-row approval action | Add checkbox selection for bulk approve/reject operations | 3 |
| H4: Consistency and standards | Table column spacing varies | Dashboard and Audit Log tables use different column widths | Standardise table layouts with consistent spacing across pages | 2 |
| H5: Error prevention | Company disable action exposed | Partners/Company page shows "Company Disabled" button at bottom | Red warning section visible but add confirmation modal requirement | 4 |
| H6: Recognition rather than recall | Company details require navigation | Admin must click into each company to view full verification info | Current profile page shows complete details \- navigation necessary | 2 |
| H8: Aesthetic and minimalist design | Dashboard shows 4 chart types | Analytics Overview displays bar, line, area, and trend graphs | Simplify to 2 primary charts with tabs for additional metrics | 2 |
| H9: Pre-empt User Errors | Approval decision criteria missing | Partners table shows pending companies but no verification checklist | Add approval guidelines sidebar with domain verification steps | 3 |

##### 2.4.1.4 Recruiter (Silver Tier) 

| Heuristic | Problem | Original Design | Solution | \# |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| H1: Visibility of system status | Silver Tier lacks an explanation of the features they have access to | Dashboard shows "Silver" badge with no tooltip or explanation of limitations | Add info icon next to badge: "Silver: Job board only. Upgrade for CV library" | 3 |
| H2: Match between system and real world | The job salary field has no guidance | Job Upload shows "Job Salary" dropdown with "Currency", but no amount format examples | Add a placeholder showing "e.g. £40,000-50,000" below the currency selector | 2 |
| H3: User control and freedom | Cannot save incomplete job posts | The Job Upload form shows "Cancel" and "Upload" buttons only | Add "Save Draft" button between Cancel and Upload | 3 |
| H4: Consistency and standards | Badge styling inconsistent | Dashboard bottom shows "Silver", "Accepted", "Pending", "Declined" badges using different colours and sizes | Standardise badge sizing and colour scheme across all status indicators | 2 |
| H5: Error prevention | Close job has no safeguard | The Job Posting page shows a red "Close Job" button with no confirmation visible | Add confirmation: "Close posting? Applicants can no longer apply" | 4 |
| H7: Flexibility and efficiency of use | Applicant actions require individual clicks | Applicant List shows "Shortlist" buttons per row, no bulk operations | Add row checkboxes with "Export Selected", enabling bulk export | 3 |
| H8: Aesthetic and minimalist design | Dashboard chart unlabelled | Profile page displays a chart with no title or axis labels | Added ‘Recruitment Pipeline’ | 2 |
| H9: Help users recognise, diagnose and recover from errors | Required job fields are unmarked | Job Upload shows "Job Description" and salary, but no asterisk indicating required | Add a red asterisk (\*) next to required fields with a legend at the form top | 2 |

##### 2.4.1.4 Recruiter (Gold\*/Platinum) 

***\*Figma prototype currently uses ‘Platinum’ wording for simplicity. Feature access will be the same across Gold & Platinum tiers***

| Heuristic | Problem | Original Design | Solution | \# |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| H1: Visibility of system status | Dashboard metrics unlabelled | Dashboard shows "128", "4", "2" with a bar chart, but the numbers lack clear labels explaining what they represent | Add metric labels above numbers: "Total Views", "Active Jobs",, "Pending Responses" | 4 |
| H2: Match between system and real world | Search experience filter technical | Search Candidates shows "Experience: 0-2y, 3-5y, 5+y" using technical abbreviations | Replace with: "Graduate (0-2y)", "Mid-level (3-5y)", "Senior (5+y)" | 3 |
| H3: User control and freedom | Cannot clear multiple filters | Search Candidates displays active filter pills (React, Figma, Python, London) but requires removing each individually | Add "Clear All" button next to Search to reset all filters at once | 2 |
| H4: Consistency and standards | Card layouts differ across pages | Saved Favorites cards show name/role/company while Anonymous Previews cards show skills/education/location | Standardise candidate card format across Saved Favorites, Search, and Anonymous Previews | 2 |
| H5: Error prevention | Save favourite has no confirmation | The Saved Favourites star icon can be clicked to remove but no undo or confirmation | Add confirmation tooltip: "Remove from favourites?" with undo option | 2 |
| H6: Recognition rather than recall | Job Analytics charts unlabelled | Job Analytics shows "Conversion Funnel" and "Source of Hire" visualisations but no axis labels or legends | Add percentage labels to funnel stages, legend for pie chart colour coding | 3 |
| H9: Pre-empt user errors | Required fields unmarked in Create Job | The Create Job form shows "Job Name", "Job Description", "Job Salary" but missed required field indicators. | Add red asterisk (*) to required fields with "* Required" legend at top | 3 |
| H10: Help & documentation | Anonymous Previews purpose unclear | Anonymous Previews page shows candidate cards but no explanation of what information is hidden vs visible | Add info banner: "Preview shows: Skills, Education, Location. Hidden: Name, Contact until access granted" | 4 |

#### 2.4.2 Usability Testing

**Evaluation Method:** Due to the static wireframe format, usability evaluation was completed via  3 team-members simulating task execution to predict completion barriers. Evaluation assessed effectiveness, efficiency, safety, utility, learnability and memorability across student, recruiter and admin workflows. 

## 3\. Project Management

**Project Status:** On track

**2-week plan:** Draft High-Level Design document by 13-Feb, set up Github ticketing for project management, split target tech work into 5-10 smaller components, identify owners, start development work and draft Low-Level Design Documents.

**Contributions:**

**Chengyu Yang:** Responsible for the admin site map, personas, sketches, wireframes, and Figma designs.

**Sadhana Jayakumar:** Led student-related sitemap and figma designs, established local development environment and validated codebase, and finished all student view pages in Figma.

**Lienqi Peng:** Co-led Recruiter, Gold/Platinum site map. Led Figma designs, paper sketches and Wireframes.

**Gian Rajaram:** Co-Led Recruiter, Gold/Platinum site map. Completed Recruiter persona, supported heuristic/usability evaluation.

**Adam Benmohamed:** Recruiter Silver Site map, involved in creating a lot of base designs for the Figma recruiter page, involving Silver, Gold and Platinum tiers, also helped refine the overall layout and design of it.
